message:
fix(wire-protocol): use local receipt clock for stale-agent pruning instead of trusting remote clocks

The Aggregator previously used the sender-reported `sent_at_ms` from
wire envelopes for capacity eviction and stale-session pruning. This
is unsafe in a multi-agent distributed environment because remote
clocks can skew arbitrarily — a sender with a far-future clock would
prevent its session from ever being pruned, while a far-past clock
could cause premature eviction of other sessions.

Changes:
- New `ingest_envelope_at(envelope, received_at_ms)` method that takes
  an explicit local receipt timestamp for all liveness decisions
- Existing `ingest_envelope()` and `ingest_bytes()` now delegate to
  `ingest_envelope_at()` with the local `epoch_ms_now()` as the
  receipt clock
- `last_seen_ms` on AgentSession now tracks local receipt time, not
  sender-reported time, fixing the stale-agent pruning window to be
  relative to the aggregator host's own clock

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
